name: Merge Unified Integration API
what_it_does: Unified API platform providing a single normalized API for 200+ integrations across HR, ATS, CRM, accounting, ticketing, and file storage — enabling B2B SaaS products to offer native integrations without building individual connectors.
use_cases: Reading employee data from any HRIS (Workday, ADP, BambooHR) via single Merge API
Syncing candidate data from multiple ATS platforms through unified candidates endpoint
Building CRM integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot) without managing individual OAuth flows
Accounting integrations (QuickBooks, Xero, Sage) via normalized ledger data model
Offering customers native file storage integrations (Drive, Box, Dropbox) via single API
not_for: Internal tooling integrations (Merge targets B2B SaaS offering integrations to customers)
Real-time streaming data — Merge syncs on schedule, not in real-time
Custom integration logic requiring raw API access (Merge normalizes to common model)
best_when: A B2B SaaS team needs to offer multiple integrations to customers without building each connector individually, accepting the normalized data model tradeoff for speed.
avoid_when: You need real-time data streaming, custom fields outside the normalized model, or are building internal tools rather than customer-facing integrations.

auth.notes: Account token (your API key) + linked account token (per customer integration). Two-level auth: your app auth + end-user linked account auth. OAuth2 flow for linking customer accounts. Link tokens for customer onboarding.

pricing.paid_tiers: Launch from $650/month; Professional from $1,500; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Enterprise-focused pricing. Per-linked-account pricing model. Significant cost but eliminates need to build and maintain 200+ individual integrations. Strong ROI for B2B SaaS teams.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; sync happens on schedule (not real-time)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent API documentation with normalized data model reference. Multi-SDK support. Webhook support for sync completion events. Passthrough API for accessing raw underlying API when normalized model is insufficient.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All read operations idempotent — Merge handles sync. Linked account management operations are idempotent. Data returned is from Merge's synced copy — eventual consistency with source systems.
known_agent_gotchas: Two-level auth — must manage both account token and linked account token per customer
Data synced on schedule — not real-time; check sync frequency for your plan
Normalized model may miss custom fields — use passthrough API for edge cases
Customer must complete OAuth flow via Merge Link before data is available

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Two-level token auth. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, and HIPAA compliant. US and EU data residency. Customer credentials stored by Merge — verify data handling policies.
